Title: Patient dashboard header displays given and family name on load
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; an active patient with name Maria Tester exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search for the patient by name | 2. Open the patient dashboard | 3. Observe the demographics header
Expected_Result: The dashboard header shows the full name "Maria Tester" exactly as registered

Title: Preferred patient identifier is displayed in the demographics banner
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with OpenMRS ID 10000X exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Locate the identifier region in the banner
Expected_Result: The preferred identifier 10000X and its type label are shown in the banner

Title: Gender and birthdate render in the demographics summary
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient DOB 15-Jan-1990, Female exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the gender and birthdate fields
Expected_Result: Gender shows Female and birthdate shows 15 Jan 1990 in the configured display format

Title: Age computed correctly from birthdate for an adult patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date is 01-Jul-2026; patient DOB 01-Jul-1990
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the displayed age
Expected_Result: Age displays as 36 year(s)

Title: Age shown one year less the day before a birthday
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date is 30-Jun-2026; patient DOB 01-Jul-1990
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the displayed age
Expected_Result: Age displays as 35 year(s), not 36

Title: Infant age displays in months when under one year
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date 01-Jul-2026; patient DOB 01-Apr-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the displayed age
Expected_Result: Age displays in months (3 month(s)) rather than 0 years

Title: Neonate age displays in days when only days old
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date 05-Jul-2026; patient DOB 01-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the displayed age
Expected_Result: Age displays in days (4 day(s))

Title: Leap-day birthdate handled in a non-leap year
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date 28-Feb-2027; patient DOB 29-Feb-2000
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Read the displayed age on 28-Feb-2027
Expected_Result: Age computes consistently (26 year(s)) without error and increments correctly on 01-Mar

Title: Estimated birthdate flag reflected in age display
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient registered with estimated age and birthdateEstimated=true
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Inspect the age/birthdate presentation
Expected_Result: Age is shown and the dashboard conveys the birthdate is estimated (approx indicator)

Title: Show Contact Info expands address and phone details
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has address and phone attributes populated
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Click "Show Contact Info" | 3. Observe the expanded panel
Expected_Result: The contact panel expands and displays the registered address and phone number

Title: Multi-line address fields render in correct order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has full address1, address2, city, state, postal, country
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Click Show Contact Info | 3. Read each address line
Test_Data: Address1=10 Main St; Address2=Apt 4; City=Boston; State=MA; Postal=02118; Country=USA
Expected_Result: Address lines display in the configured address template order with no missing fields

Title: Phone number attribute shows when present and hidden when absent
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; two patients exist, one with phone and one without
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient A (has phone) and Show Contact Info | 2. Open patient B (no phone) and Show Contact Info
Expected_Result: Patient A shows the phone value; Patient B shows blank or omits the phone row without error

Title: Edit Registration Information opens the registration edit form
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk with edit privilege; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Click General Actions | 2. Select "Edit Registration Information" | 3. Observe the form
Expected_Result: The registration edit form loads pre-populated with the patient current demographics

Title: Edited family name persists and updates the dashboard banner
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient Maria Tester open in edit form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Edit Registration Information | 2. Change family name to Tester-Smith | 3. Save/Confirm | 4. Return to dashboard
Expected_Result: The change is saved and the banner reflects "Maria Tester-Smith"

Title: Cancelling edit discards changes without persisting
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient open in edit form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Edit Registration Information | 2. Change city to Cambridge | 3. Click Cancel | 4. Reopen Show Contact Info
Test_Data: City changed to Cambridge then cancelled
Expected_Result: No change is saved; the original city is retained

Title: Reject future birthdate during edit
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient open in edit form; system date 01-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open Edit Registration Information | 2. Set birthdate to 01-Jul-2030 | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Validation error is shown and the future birthdate is not saved

Title: Reject empty required given/family name on save
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient open in edit form
Test_Steps: 1. Open Edit Registration Information | 2. Clear the family name field | 3. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: A required-field validation error appears and the record is not saved with an empty name

Title: Mark Patient Deceased with date and cause updates patient state
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with mark-deceased privilege; living patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Click General Actions | 2. Select Mark Patient Deceased | 3. Enter death date | 4. Select cause of death | 5. Confirm
Expected_Result: Patient is flagged deceased; the banner shows a deceased indicator and the recorded death date

Title: Death date cannot be before birthdate
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient DOB 15-Jan-1990 open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Enter death date 15-Jan-1980 | 3. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: Validation prevents saving a death date earlier than the birthdate

Title: Death date cannot be in the future
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; system date 01-Jul-2026; living patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Enter death date 01-Aug-2026 | 3. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: Validation prevents saving a future death date

Title: Cause of death is required when marking deceased
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; living patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Enter a valid death date | 3. Leave cause of death empty | 4. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: Validation requires a cause of death; the action is not completed

Title: Other cause requires free-text non-coded value
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; living patient open; Other Non-Coded cause configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Mark Patient Deceased | 2. Enter death date | 3. Select cause Other | 4. Leave the non-coded text empty | 5. Attempt confirm
Expected_Result: When Other is selected, the free-text field is required and validated before saving

Title: Deceased banner styling and date persist after reload
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already marked deceased on 20-Jun-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient dashboard | 2. Reload the page | 3. Observe banner
Expected_Result: The deceased state persists across reload with the death date visible

Title: Revert a patient from deceased back to living
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with privilege; patient currently marked deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Open General Actions on deceased patient | 2. Choose edit/undo deceased flow | 3. Set patient back to alive | 4. Confirm | 5. Observe banner
Expected_Result: Patient returns to living state, deceased indicator and death date are removed

Title: Mark Deceased action hidden for user without privilege
Preconditions: Logged in as a read-only Data Clerk lacking mark-deceased privilege; living patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Open General Actions menu | 3. Inspect available actions
Expected_Result: Mark Patient Deceased option is not displayed for the unprivileged user

Title: Edit Registration hidden without registration edit privilege
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician without registration-edit privilege; patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open General Actions menu | 2. Inspect available actions
Expected_Result: Edit Registration Information is not offered to the unprivileged user

Title: Privileged user sees full General Actions set
Preconditions: Logged in as Admin with all dashboard privileges; patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open General Actions menu | 2. Enumerate visible actions
Expected_Result: All configured actions including Edit Registration and Mark Deceased are visible

Title: Direct URL access to edit form blocked without privilege
Preconditions: Logged in as unprivileged Clinician; know the edit registration form URL/path
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate directly to the edit registration URL for the patient | 2. Observe the response
Test_Data: Role lacks edit privilege; direct edit URL
Expected_Result: Access is denied/redirected; server enforces privilege independent of UI hiding

Title: Attachments widget lists existing patient attachments
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has at least one uploaded attachment; attachments module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Locate the Attachments widget | 3. Observe listed items
Expected_Result: The Attachments widget displays the existing attachment with a thumbnail/label

Title: Upload a valid image attachment to the patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician with upload privilege; attachments module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open Attachments widget | 2. Click upload | 3. Select a JPG under the size limit | 4. Add caption | 5. Confirm upload
Expected_Result: The image uploads and appears in the widget with its caption

Title: Reject upload of disallowed executable file type
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; attachments module enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open Attachments widget | 2. Attempt to upload malware.exe
Expected_Result: Upload is rejected with a file-type validation error and no file is stored

Title: Reject attachment exceeding maximum size
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; max upload size configured to 5 MB
Test_Steps: 1. Open Attachments widget | 2. Attempt to upload a 6 MB file
Expected_Result: Upload is rejected with a size-limit error

Title: Long given name does not truncate critical identifiers in banner
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with a very long compound name exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dashboard for the long-named patient | 2. Inspect banner layout
Expected_Result: Name wraps or ellipsizes gracefully while patient ID remains fully visible

Title: Unicode and diacritic names render correctly
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with non-ASCII name exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dashboard | 2. Inspect the rendered name
Expected_Result: Diacritic and Unicode characters render without mojibake or substitution

Title: Patient with multiple identifiers shows preferred one prominently
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has OpenMRS ID (preferred) and a secondary identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard | 2. Inspect identifier display
Expected_Result: The preferred identifier is shown prominently; secondary identifiers are accessible but not primary

Title: REST API returns patient demographics for valid UUID
Preconditions: REST session authenticated; valid patient UUID known
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} | 2. Inspect JSON body
Expected_Result: HTTP 200 with person display, gender, birthdate, and preferred identifier in the response

Title: REST API returns 404 for non-existent patient UUID
Preconditions: REST session authenticated
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} with a random UUID | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 404 with an error payload and no patient data leaked

Title: Unauthenticated demographics request is rejected
Preconditions: No valid session/credentials
Test_Steps: 1. Send GET /ws/rest/v1/patient/{uuid} with no auth header | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: HTTP 401/403; no PHI returned to the anonymous caller

Title: FHIR Patient resource exposes name, gender, birthDate
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled and authenticated; patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET [base]/Patient/{id} with Accept application/fhir+json | 2. Validate resource
Expected_Result: Resource type Patient with HumanName, administrative gender, and birthDate populated per FHIR R4

Title: FHIR Patient deceased[x] reflects deceased status and datetime
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient marked deceased on 20-Jun-2026
Test_Steps: 1. GET [base]/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect deceasedDateTime
Expected_Result: deceasedDateTime is present and equals the recorded death date in FHIR datetime format

Title: FHIR Patient.identifier uses a valid system URI
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient with OpenMRS ID exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET [base]/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect identifier.system and value
Expected_Result: Each identifier has a non-empty system URI and value; identifier use/type coding is well-formed

Title: FHIR Patient.telecom and address map from demographics
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient has phone and address
Test_Steps: 1. GET [base]/Patient/{id} | 2. Inspect telecom and address arrays
Expected_Result: telecom phone and address city/state map correctly from the source demographics

Title: ADT^A08 update reflects demographic change on dashboard
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; patient exists; inbound ADT processing enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A08 with updated address | 2. Allow processing | 3. Open dashboard Show Contact Info
Test_Data: PID address updated to 22 Oak Ave
Expected_Result: The demographic update from the HL7 message is reflected in the dashboard contact info

Title: ADT^A23 deceased indicator maps to deceased state
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured; living patient exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT message with PID-30 deceased indicator Y and PID-29 death date | 2. Process | 3. Open dashboard
Expected_Result: Patient is shown deceased with the death date matching PID-29

Title: Death date stored is consistent with displayed value
Preconditions: DB read access; patient marked deceased via UI on 20-Jun-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Mark patient deceased in UI | 2. Query person.death_date and dead flag | 3. Compare with dashboard
Expected_Result: person.dead=1 and person.death_date equals the UI-entered date with no timezone drift

Title: Voided demographic fields are not displayed on dashboard
Preconditions: DB access; a person_address row marked voided exists for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm voided=1 on an address row | 2. Open dashboard Show Contact Info
Expected_Result: The voided address is excluded from the dashboard; only active address is shown

Title: Audit columns populated on demographic edit
Preconditions: DB access; Registration Clerk edits a demographic field
Test_Steps: 1. Edit family name in UI and save | 2. Query the changed_by and date_changed columns
Expected_Result: changed_by and date_changed reflect the editing user and timestamp for the change

Title: Marking deceased generates an audit trail entry
Preconditions: Audit/logging enabled; Clinician marks a patient deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Mark patient deceased | 2. Open audit log or query log store | 3. Locate the event
Expected_Result: An audit record captures the actor, patient, action, and timestamp for the deceased change

Title: Viewing patient PHI is recorded for accountability
Preconditions: Access logging enabled; Clinician opens a patient dashboard
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient dashboard | 2. Inspect the access log
Expected_Result: A PHI-access record links the user, patient, and access time

Title: Demographics banner is reachable and labeled for screen readers
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; screen reader enabled; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the demographics banner with a screen reader | 2. Verify announced content
Expected_Result: Name, gender, age, and ID are announced with meaningful labels (ARIA/landmarks)

Title: General Actions menu operable via keyboard only
Preconditions: Logged in as privileged user; patient dashboard open; no mouse
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to General Actions | 2. Open with Enter/Space | 3. Arrow through items | 4. Activate an item
Expected_Result: The menu opens, items are focusable in order, and actions activate via keyboard

Title: Deceased indicator conveys status by more than color
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; deceased patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect the deceased indicator | 2. Verify a text label or icon accompanies any color cue
Expected_Result: Deceased status is conveyed with text/icon, not color alone, meeting WCAG 1.4.1

Title: Demographic fields are escaped against stored XSS
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; able to edit a name field
Test_Steps: 1. Edit given name to a script payload | 2. Save | 3. Reopen the dashboard for the patient
Expected_Result: The payload is rendered inert as text; no script executes on the dashboard

Title: Cross-patient data isolation on dashboard
Preconditions: Two patients exist; user authorized for patient A only (or tampering attempt)
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient A dashboard | 2. Manipulate the patient identifier in the URL to patient B | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: Either access is authorized per policy or denied; no unauthorized PHI of patient B is exposed inadvertently

Title: Contact info display across gender x phone x address combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; test patients prepared for combinations
Test_Steps: 1. For each combination open dashboard and Show Contact Info | 2. Verify rendering
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination renders the present fields and omits absent ones without error

Title: Identifier x deceased x estimated-age display combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; prepared patients for combinations
Test_Steps: 1. For each combination open the dashboard | 2. Verify banner elements
Expected_Result: Banner correctly composes identifier, deceased indicator, and age across combinations

Title: Exploratory tour of dashboard widgets for broken states
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a patient with sparse data exists
Test_Steps: 1. Freely explore each dashboard widget | 2. Resize window | 3. Toggle Show Contact Info repeatedly | 4. Note anomalies
Expected_Result: No broken layouts, console errors, or stuck spinners; anomalies are documented

Title: Exploratory edit/save race and rapid navigation
Preconditions: Logged in as Registration Clerk; patient open
Test_Steps: 1. Open edit form | 2. Rapidly navigate away and back | 3. Submit edit twice quickly | 4. Observe
Expected_Result: No duplicate persists and no inconsistent state; system handles concurrent submit safely

Title: Clinical actions restricted appropriately after deceased state
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient just marked deceased
Test_Steps: 1. Mark patient deceased | 2. Attempt to start a new active visit/encounter
Expected_Result: Workflow appropriately warns or restricts new active clinical activity for a deceased patient

Title: Dashboard loads within performance budget for a typical patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; representative patient with attachments and history
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger dashboard load | 2. Measure time to interactive
Expected_Result: Dashboard becomes interactive within the agreed budget (e.g. under 3 seconds) on the reference environment

Title: Adapter layer maps omiiCARE patient ID into banner
Preconditions: omiiCARE platform via Resource Adapter Layer; patient sourced from omiiCARE
Test_Steps: 1. Open dashboard for an omiiCARE-sourced patient | 2. Inspect the identifier display
Expected_Result: The adapter-mapped identifier and type render correctly in the banner

Title: Concurrent edit by two users surfaces a conflict safeguard
Preconditions: Two Registration Clerks open the same patient edit form simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. User A edits city and saves | 2. User B (stale form) edits state and saves | 3. Observe outcome
Expected_Result: The system prevents silent overwrite or clearly reflects last-write with audit; no data corruption

Title: Show Contact Info collapses back to hidden state
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with contact info open
Test_Steps: 1. Click Show Contact Info to expand | 2. Click again/Hide | 3. Observe
Expected_Result: The panel toggles closed and contact details are hidden again, returning to initial state

Title: Deceased patient age reflects age at death not current age
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient DOB 01-Jan-1950, deceased 01-Jan-2020; today 01-Jul-2026
Test_Steps: 1. Open the deceased patient dashboard | 2. Read age presentation
Expected_Result: Age is presented as age at death (70) or clearly annotated, not the current-date age
